title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Access Points, and Tenant Isolation
description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A bucket is private by convention, one application role has s3:*, tenant prefixes are interpolated from user input, and ACLs conflict with ownership and public-block assumptions. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or. Learners cumulatively produce least-privilege TypeScript authorization proof through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
